steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks what "breathes" most nearly means as used in the poem. The decisive clue is the context: the name "breathes bravery and life and courage to the tribe." The verb must describe how the name sends or conveys these abstract qualities outward to others.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Imparts': Means to pass on or bestow something abstract (knowledge, virtue, a quality) to others — exactly how the name conveys bravery and courage to the tribe. ✓
  • Choice B — 'Renounces': Means to formally give up or reject; a name cannot reject the qualities it is said to communicate outward. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Assents': Means to agree or consent; a name does not agree to something, and the word implies a two-party exchange that is absent here. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Absorbs': Means to take something in; this is the opposite direction — the name sends qualities out to the tribe rather than drawing qualities inward. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
